method: exhaustive integer grid/line search over actionable loan terms, pinned model
note: Recourse = a valid integer change to loan duration and/or credit amount reaching P(bad)<=1/6 (deployed accept). Grid is EVERY integer in the train range, so 'infeasible' means no loan-term change works (decline rests on non-actionable factors). Reason-code attribution uses a range-normalised minimal change (months vs DM comparable). NB: an initial DiCE random search found only 72.5% recourse (a superseded run, not recomputed here) — off-the-shelf CF tools can conflate search-failure with infeasibility. Real-world feasibility (can an applicant borrow less?) is not assessed.
